behavior: Secretive and often solitary outside the breeding season, usually remaining low in dense cover and foraging on or near the ground for insects and other invertebrates; berries and other fruit are also taken seasonally. Males deliver a powerful, varied song by day and night during the breeding season and defend territories with song and pursuit.
field_marks: Plain warm-brown upperparts and unmarked folded wings
Broad rusty-chestnut tail contrasting with the browner back
Pale buff-to-whitish throat, breast, and belly
Large dark eye with a narrow pale eye-ring
Slim, straight dark bill with a warmer base to the lower mandible
Compact, upright proportions with slender pinkish-brown legs
habitat: Dense low scrub, tangled thickets, woodland edges, and young or open woodland with a shaded understory and patches of bare ground; nests are placed on or near the ground within thick vegetation.
